stories.name: Bi-directional skill↔CLI drift test
acceptanceCriteria: New test file crates/cli/tests/skill_drift.rs exists.
Test extracts every `rutracker …` command mentioned in .claude/skills/rutracker.md (any subcommand depth) into a set A.
Test extracts every clap subcommand path from the built rutracker binary by walking `--help` output recursively into a set B.
Assertion 1 (no extras in skill): A \ B is empty — every command in the skill must exist in the CLI.
Assertion 2 (no gaps in skill): B \ A is empty UNLESS the command appears in a documented allowlist in the same test file (with a 1-line rationale per entry).
Test uses CARGO_BIN_EXE_rutracker to locate the built binary — no cargo-install step required.
Test name `test_skill_commands_match_cli_surface` appears in `cargo test --workspace` output and passes.
cargo test --workspace / clippy / fmt all clean.

stories.name: GitHub Actions CI
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and declares jobs: test, clippy, fmt, coverage.
CI triggers: on push to main, on pull_request against main.
Rust toolchain: stable, with dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable action + caching via Swatinem/rust-cache.
test job runs `cargo test --workspace --locked` on ubuntu-latest AND macos-latest; uses --locked to catch Cargo.lock drift.
clippy job runs `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings`.
fmt job runs `cargo fmt --all -- --check`.
coverage job uses cargo-llvm-cov, fails the build if coverage drops below 95% line coverage.
Workflow syntax is valid (`actionlint` parses without error OR local GitHub CLI `gh workflow view` succeeds; verify with `python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/ci.yml"))'`).
Badge in README.md pointing at the workflow status.
Workflow runs green on at least one commit after push to origin/main (verified via `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 1 --json status,conclusion` showing `completed`/`success`).

stories.name: Coverage tooling + documented 100% (≥95% effective, gaps justified)
acceptanceCriteria: cargo-llvm-cov installed locally; README 'Development' section documents `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lcov --output-path lcov.info`.
A baseline coverage report is generated; current line coverage % is recorded in progress.txt.
New tests added where gaps exist; target ≥95% line coverage workspace-wide (pragmatic 100% with documented exclusions).

Paths that CANNOT be 100%-unit-tested are explicitly excluded via `#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]` or `// LCOV_EXCL_*` markers, each with a one-line rationale comment. Known exclusions: live macOS Keychain I/O in cookies-macos/src/keychain.rs, the MCP stdio loop's EOF path in mcp/src/main.rs, any `#[ignore]`-d live tests.
A llvm-cov.toml (or .config/nextest.toml-equivalent) file or cargo-llvm-cov CLI flags configured to treat these exclusions consistently in CI.
`cargo llvm-cov --workspace --summary-only` reports ≥95% line coverage; the exact number goes into progress.txt.
CI coverage job enforces the same ≥95% threshold (see US-007).

stories.name: CI is green on remote
acceptanceCriteria: Local `cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --all -- --check && cargo llvm-cov --workspace --summary-only` all exit 0 AND coverage ≥ 95%.
All local changes committed and pushed to origin/main.
`gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 1 --json status,conclusion` reports conclusion=success for the latest run.
If CI fails, fixes are applied, re-pushed, and the run is re-checked until green.
